headline: Sex Education Remains Taboo Despite 14,000 Teen Pregnancies Annually
context: Comprehensive sexuality education is not taught in Malaysian schools while teenage pregnancy rates remain stubbornly high.

cards.big: 14,000 teenage pregnancies recorded annually with youngest cases aged 12 in 2025
cards.sub: Schools teach only abstinence within existing health education modules

cards.big: MOE Reproductive Health module covers anatomy but excludes contraception and consent
cards.sub: Content restrictions mean students receive incomplete health information

cards.big: 86% of parents in 2025 LPPKN survey support age-appropriate sexuality education in schools
cards.sub: Political resistance rather than parental opposition blocks curriculum reform

cards.big: UNESCO evidence shows comprehensive sex education reduces teen pregnancy by 30-50%
cards.sub: Countries implementing CSE see measurable declines in unintended pregnancies

cards.big: Withholding health information from teenagers does not protect them—it endangers them
Should Malaysia introduce age-appropriate comprehensive sexuality education in secondary schools
